sources.title: Assigning issues and pull requests to other GitHub users
positive_evidence: Documents that direct assignment depends on repository permissions
limits: Does not define a community claim-lease protocol

sources.title: Managing access to your projects
sources.source_role: Public visibility and project edit-permission boundary
positive_evidence: Separates public visibility from project edit access
limits: Project permissions do not replace repository permissions

sources.title: Configuring issue templates for your repository
positive_evidence: Provides maintained YAML issue-form contracts
limits: Submitting a form is not readiness or authorization

sources.title: About code owners
positive_evidence: Provides path-based review ownership
limits: Enforcement also depends on repository branch rules

sources.title: Creating discussion category forms
positive_evidence: Defines category-slug YAML forms for public Discussions
limits: Submitting a Discussion form does not admit Ready work or grant authority

sources.title: About discussions
positive_evidence: Documents Discussions as an open-ended place for a community to discuss project direction and future, plan, and gather input
limits: A Discussion post is conversation, not an admitted repository decision or execution authority

sources.title: Managing discussions
positive_evidence: Documents that maintainers can create an Issue when a Discussion yields an idea or bug that is ready to be worked on
limits: Creating an Issue does not by itself prove readiness, correctness, or permission

sources.title: Secure use reference
positive_evidence: Documents script injection, third-party action, and credential risks
limits: Repository-specific threat review remains necessary

sources.title: Use GITHUB_TOKEN for authentication in workflows
positive_evidence: Supports explicit, minimized workflow permissions
limits: A read-only token does not make executed contribution code trusted

sources.title: REST API endpoints for rules
sources.source_role: Machine interface for repository ruleset inspection and maintenance
positive_evidence: Documents create, read, update, history, and branch-rule endpoints
limits: API capability does not authorize an agent or contributor to change repository settings

sources.title: Available rules for rulesets
sources.source_role: Normative rule semantics for protected branches
positive_evidence: Documents pull-request, status-check, thread-resolution, deletion, and force-push rules
limits: Required-review counts must fit the repository's actual maintainer topology

sources.title: Agentic Tools
positive_evidence: Publishes agent discovery instructions and XR device tooling for multiple coding assistants
limits: Reference precedent only; no code or permission model is inherited

sources.title: Multica open-source managed agents platform
positive_evidence: Implements issue assignment, execution tracking, reusable skills, local runtimes, and support for Codex, Cursor, Claude Code, and other coding tools
limits: Multica's authenticated server database and agent permissions are not XR Foundry's public GitHub authority

sources.title: Tasks
positive_evidence: Separates durable issues from execution tasks, records task states and runs, distinguishes infrastructure retry from a fresh manual rerun, and documents provider session-resume capabilities
limits: Quota and agent errors are non-retryable, and a private provider session does not create a cross-provider public handoff

sources.title: Assign issues to agents
sources.source_role: Agent assignment, reassignment, and concurrency precedent
positive_evidence: Documents routed agent tasks, explicit task lifecycle, one active task per agent per issue, reassignment, and parallel agents on one issue
limits: Reassignment cancels active issue tasks and assigned agents can edit issue state; XR Foundry must map execution to bounded checkpoints and preserve maintainer authority

sources.title: Project resources
positive_evidence: Documents Git worktree and local-directory modes, per-directory serialization, resource scoping, and structured project context for Agents
limits: Local-directory mode has no file-level lock, dirty-tree protection, automatic commit, or pull request and may write Agent guidance files; it requires a separate security and authority adapter before use

sources.title: Encouraging helpful contributions to your project with labels
positive_evidence: Documents that good first issue labels help people find approachable work and can be surfaced by GitHub
limits: A discoverability label does not prove readiness, reserve work, or grant repository permission

sources.title: Setting guidelines for repository contributors
positive_evidence: Documents where GitHub surfaces CONTRIBUTING guidance and how it can communicate contribution routes
limits: Guidance text does not enforce acceptance, validation, or authority

sources.title: Profile contributions reference
sources.source_role: Boundary of GitHub's built-in public contribution display
positive_evidence: Documents which repository activities GitHub can count or display on a profile
limits: A profile activity count does not measure contribution quality, non-code impact, trust, or repository authority

sources.title: Viewing a project's contributors
sources.source_role: Boundary of GitHub's repository contributor graph
positive_evidence: Documents how GitHub derives the contributors graph from repository commits
limits: Commit-derived visibility omits many review, research, testing, mentoring, and device contributions

sources.title: Kubernetes Contributor Guide
sources.source_role: Mature public work-discovery and contribution-path precedent
positive_evidence: Publishes how contributors find work, file Issues, and submit pull requests in a large community project
limits: Kubernetes commands, bots, ownership, and organization membership are not inherited

sources.title: Help Wanted and Good First Issue labels
sources.source_role: Maintainer-curated newcomer and external-help task precedent
positive_evidence: Defines extra context and mentoring expectations for help-wanted and good-first-issue work
limits: Kubernetes label automation and staffing assumptions are not inherited

sources.title: Kubernetes Enhancement Proposals
sources.source_role: Versioned, reviewable proposal and decision-history precedent
positive_evidence: Describes a common proposal format, explicit reviewers and approvers, searchable status, and a discoverable historical decision record for non-trivial changes
limits: XR Foundry does not inherit Kubernetes governance, approval roles, or mandatory process for already-bounded low-risk work

sources.title: Roles and responsibilities
positive_evidence: Distinguishes contributor, member, reviewer, and approver duties with increasing responsibility
limits: XR Foundry needs its own role criteria and explicit maintainer decisions

sources.title: Project groups
sources.source_role: Bounded public project-charter and completion precedent
positive_evidence: Describes scoped project groups with leads, liaisons, members, repositories, and archival after completion
limits: Does not define XR Foundry checkpoint or continuation-handoff data

sources.title: Issue assignment
sources.source_role: Public claim, release, and reassignment precedent
positive_evidence: Documents explicit assignment, release, and reassignment flows for issue work
limits: XR Foundry V1 does not install or trust a comment-trigger assignment bot

sources.title: Review queue tracking
sources.source_role: Review capacity and explicit reviewer-load precedent
positive_evidence: Documents explicit review queues and reviewer capacity tracking
limits: A queue does not make an Agent review equivalent to a required human approval

sources.title: How to contribute
positive_evidence: Recognizes code, review, testing, documentation, translation, issue, demo, and community contributions
limits: Godot's engine, team, and review workflow do not define Unity package acceptance

sources.title: Bug triage guidelines
sources.source_role: Public triage, stale-state, and adoption precedent
positive_evidence: Documents structured triage and explicit follow-up for work that needs reproduction or contributor input
limits: Godot triage labels do not define XR Foundry lease or continuation authority

sources.title: Pull request review process
sources.source_role: Focused change, review, testing, and draft-state precedent
positive_evidence: Recommends small focused pull requests, testing before review, draft state for unresolved failures, and community review and test feedback
limits: Small pull requests can still need explicit dependency and integration coordination

sources.title: Incentivizing contributors to move up the ladder
positive_evidence: Recommends explicit roles, responsibilities, privileges, effort expectations, and traceable recognition
limits: Mentions ranked activity only as an option and does not require a leaderboard or score-based promotion

sources.title: Project Health Measurement
positive_evidence: Recommends a small project-specific set of actionable health measures and a reality check on imperfect dashboards
limits: Popularity and raw activity measures do not by themselves establish project health or individual trust

sources.title: All Contributors specification
positive_evidence: Recommends recognizing all contribution categories, linking contributors, and treating contributor ordering as immaterial
limits: The specification does not validate repository-specific evidence or grant roles

sources.title: Emoji Key and Contribution Types
positive_evidence: Publishes distinct categories for code and non-code contribution acknowledgement
limits: XR Foundry uses a smaller typed vocabulary and public evidence contract

sources.title: Metric: Contributors
positive_evidence: Defines contributors broadly across code and non-code work and documents multiple collection routes
limits: Warns that publishing metrics can create privacy, contractual, and data-ethics risks

sources.title: Kernel Guidelines for Tool-Generated Content
sources.source_role: Human accountability and transparent tool-origin precedent
positive_evidence: Requires submitters to understand and defend tool-generated contributions and encourages transparent disclosure of tools, affected scope, and testing
limits: Linux kernel licensing, mailing-list, and maintainer policies are not inherited

sources.title: Submitting patches: Using Assisted-by
positive_evidence: Documents Assisted-by disclosure for advanced coding tools while human sign-off remains separate
limits: The trailer records assistance and does not make a tool a human author, reviewer, or legal signatory

sources.title: Review AI-generated code
sources.source_role: Human oversight and validation for AI-generated changes
positive_evidence: Emphasizes functional checks, architecture and intent review, dependency scrutiny, collaborative review, and human oversight
limits: The guidance does not guarantee correctness of AI output or feedback

sources.title: Using GitHub Copilot code review
positive_evidence: Documents that Copilot leaves comments rather than approvals and its review does not count toward required approvals
limits: AI findings still require validation and do not replace human review

sources.title: Open Source Project Security Baseline, 2026-02-19
positive_evidence: OSPS-QA-05.01 states that an active version-control system must not contain generated executable artifacts
limits: Recommends build-time generation or separate storage but does not prescribe one artifact host or attestation implementation
